text: I'm not used to reading so much marketing in a README.md. not sure how I feel about that. I'm all for aspiring to revolutionary change, but a "please like and Subscribe" in that context is off-putting for me. Just me being a curmudgeon? Is everything YouTube?

elements.text: I'm not used to reading so much marketing in a README.md. not sure how I feel about that. I'm all for aspiring to revolutionary change, but a "please like and Subscribe" in that context is off-putting for me. Just me being a curmudgeon? Is everything YouTube?

text: Jason, you're a curmudgeon! I love the richness of the README, I wish others could take so much care over theirs! (Now Medet's gonna tell me it was spun up by ChatGPT...)

elements.text: Jason, you're a curmudgeon! I love the richness of the README, I wish others could take so much care over theirs! (Now Medet's gonna tell me it was spun up by ChatGPT...)

text:
Most of your examples for "misaligned AI" are actually examples of misaligned human users of AI. People who either pursue goals that are bad for the rest of us, or who let bad things happen through negligence.

I'd say our problem is not "An AI with the strategic brilliance of Sun Tzu, the patience of a tortoise, and the moral compass of a drunk pirate", but rather "An AI with the strategic brilliance of Sun Tzu, and the patience of a tortoise, in the hands of a drunk pirate."
